response.justification: The role requires 8 years of C++ programming as a hard minimum qualification, and the candidate's resume shows only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a bachelor thesis. The candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and there is no evidence of leading a C++ cloud compute platform. While there is genuine distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic) and strong teaching/community work, the core technical prerequisite is not met, making this a poor fit for a C++ Technical Lead role.
strengths: Distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and code katas
Long track record of senior engineering roles across web, backend, and embedded domains
concerns: Only ~6 months of C/C++ professional experience vs. the 8-year minimum
No demonstrated cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture leadership
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at scale

key_factors.factor: Missing cloud compute platform / cloud architecture background
Distributed systems and mentoring/teaching track record
